C011.summary: P000 is successful. Release Controller now has an explicit CI quality-gate model, default gate configuration and documentation, tests/guards against drift, and a live API-host deployment that enforced gates before staging build/deploy in a successful branch-poll release.

P000.description: NovAIC now has Release Controller-owned backend and LLM Factory deployment, but the quality gate inside that controller is still too thin: local developer unit tests are advisory, while Release Controller preflight only runs lightweight checks before building and deploying staging. The next correct step is to make CI quality enforcement an explicit Release Controller capability, so a pushed commit cannot become a staging release unless controller-run tests/guards pass, and prod promotion remains tied to a release that passed staging.
success_criteria: Release Controller has a clear CI quality-gate model distinct from deploy smoke and direct manual scripts.
Branch release plans run deterministic controller-owned quality gates before image build and before staging deployment.
Gate failures block image build/deploy and are recorded as failed Release Controller runs.
The default/sample configuration includes meaningful repo-level gates that can run in the controller worktree without relying on developer-local state.
Tests cover quality-gate planning, execution ordering, and failure behavior.

Documentation explains the development flow: local unit tests for fast feedback, Release Controller CI gate for authoritative staging admission, staging integration/smoke, and prod promotion from the passed release.
Existing manual backend/factory deployment rejection remains intact.
P000.title: Add Release Controller CI quality gate

P001.description: The controller currently has a generic `deploy.verify_commands` list that is not clearly modeled as CI. A clean solution needs typed quality gates with stable names, validated commands, and branch-release plan steps that run after source checkout/submodule update and before any image build or staging deploy. This child handles the code-level model, planner ordering, runner failure semantics, and unit tests.
success_criteria: Release Controller config accepts an explicit quality-gate list with stable gate names and argv commands.
Malformed quality gates and empty commands are rejected by config validation.
Branch release plans include named `quality-*` steps after git/submodule steps and before `build-api-backend` / `build-llm-factory`.
Promotion and rollback plans do not include branch CI gates or image build steps.
Nonzero quality gate exits stop subsequent build/deploy steps and surface the failed step in the release result.
Tests cover parsing, ordering, dry-run/real runner behavior, and existing manual deploy guard behavior remains intact.
P001.title: Implement first-class Release Controller quality gates

P002.description: Even after the code supports quality gates, the project needs a clear default contract: local unit tests are fast feedback, Release Controller gates are authoritative staging admission, staging smoke/integration happens after deploy, and prod promotion uses the already accepted immutable release. This child updates sample config, docs, and repository guards so the intended flow is explicit and hard to regress.
success_criteria: `novaic-release-controller/config.sample.json` includes meaningful default quality gates that run in the controller worktree.
Docs describe the canonical development and CI/CD flow from local tests through staging admission and prod promotion.
Docs distinguish quality gates, image build/import checks, staging smoke/integration checks, and prod smoke.
Repository guard tests/lints verify that quality gates are documented and sample config remains aligned with the controller model.
Manual backend/factory deploy paths remain documented as rejected internal executor paths.
P002.title: Configure and document the CI/CD quality gate flow

P003.description: The new quality-gate capability only solves the operational problem after the API-host Release Controller is upgraded and its runtime config contains the gate list. The rollout must avoid the same old-controller/new-code race: pause polling, publish the code, deploy the upgraded controller, enable gates in remote config, run a controlled staging trigger or observe polling, then confirm failures would block before build/deploy.
success_criteria: API-host polling is paused before pushing a controller change that requires the new gate model.
Code and submodule pointers for the quality-gate change are committed and pushed intentionally.
API-host Release Controller image is rebuilt/pushed/deployed from the quality-gate commit.
Remote `/opt/novaic/release-controller/config.json` includes the intended quality gates.
A staging run through Release Controller shows quality gate steps before image build/deploy and succeeds.
Release Controller status is clean, polling is re-enabled, staging remains healthy, and prod pointer is not accidentally changed unless explicitly promoted.
P003.title: Roll out Release Controller quality gates on API host

P004.description: The quality-gate implementation must be committed and pushed without accidentally including unrelated dirty workspace changes. Polling must be paused first so the old API-host controller cannot process the new commit before it is upgraded.
success_criteria: API-host Release Controller polling is paused before pushing the quality-gate commit.
Local release-controller/deploy guard tests pass before commit.
Only intended source/config/docs/test files and the active ledger are staged, with unrelated pre-existing dirty files left untouched.
The parent commit is pushed to `origin/main` and its commit id is recorded.
If any submodule changed intentionally, its commit/push is recorded; otherwise no submodule churn is introduced.
P004.title: Publish quality-gate controller code safely

P005.description: After the quality-gate commit is pushed, the running API-host controller must be upgraded to an image built from that commit and the remote runtime config must include `quality_gates`. A staging run must prove the gates execute before build/deploy and block semantics remain controller-owned.
success_criteria: A new Release Controller image is built/pushed/deployed from the quality-gate commit.
Remote `/opt/novaic/release-controller/config.json` contains the intended `quality_gates` and preserves existing registry, branch, health, and polling settings.
A staging run through Release Controller succeeds with command plan order: git/submodule, `quality-*`, preflight, build, push, deploy, smoke.
Staging public health is clean after the run.
Prod current pointer remains unchanged unless deliberately promoted.
Polling is re-enabled and Release Controller status has `last_error=null`.
Manual direct backend/factory deploy commands still fail locally before remote side effects.
P005.title: Upgrade API-host controller and verify quality gates execute

P006.description: The default `quality_gates` include pytest-based controller CI checks, but the Release Controller Docker image currently installs only the release-controller package and runtime tooling. Without pytest in the image, the API-host controller cannot execute the default gate list and staging admission would fail for an infrastructure reason rather than a code quality reason.
success_criteria: Release Controller Docker image installs the minimal test tooling required by default `quality_gates`.
Dockerfile invariant tests cover that the test tooling remains available.
Sample quality gates remain executable inside the controller worktree.
Local tests for release-controller and release-controller CI guards pass after the image change.
P006.title: Make Release Controller image capable of running quality gates

P007.description: The Release Controller image/runtime dependency gap has been fixed in commit `6a74b2ac08b199a4c2c0db54c3fc31708bd59261`, but the API-host controller has not yet been upgraded and no staging run has proven quality gates execute before image build/deploy.
success_criteria: API-host Release Controller image is built/pushed/deployed from commit `6a74b2ac08b199a4c2c0db54c3fc31708bd59261`.
Remote config includes `quality_gates` with `release-controller-ci` and `release-path-lints` while preserving existing release settings.
A staging Release Controller run succeeds for commit `6a74b2ac08b199a4c2c0db54c3fc31708bd59261`.
The staging run command plan and execution results show `quality-release-controller-ci` and `quality-release-path-lints` before build/deploy, both successful.
Staging health is clean, prod current pointer remains unchanged, polling is re-enabled with `last_error=null`, and direct manual backend/factory deploy guards still fail.
P007.title: Complete API-host quality-gate rollout

P008.description: The first real remote quality gate failed because `novaic-release-controller/tests/test_service.py` imports FastAPI/Starlette `TestClient`, which requires `httpx`. The Release Controller image now has pytest but still lacks httpx, so the default `release-controller-ci` gate cannot run the controller test suite inside the controller container.
success_criteria: Release Controller Docker image installs `httpx` alongside pytest for the default controller CI gate.
Dockerfile invariant tests cover the `httpx` dependency.
The default gate command succeeds inside the running controller container before retrying the staging rollout.
The fix is committed and pushed while polling remains disabled.

done: Added `QualityGate` model with stable lowercase slug names and argv validation.

Added duplicate gate-name rejection and malformed gate tests.
Updated planner to insert `quality-<name>` command steps in branch release plans before `build-api-backend` and `build-llm-factory`.
Added tests proving prod promotion and rollback plans do not include quality or build steps.
Added runner test proving a nonzero `quality-*` step stops later build/deploy steps.
Exported `QualityGate` from the release-controller package.

R000.summary: Implemented the code-level Release Controller quality gate model. Controller config now has typed `quality_gates`; branch release plans render them as named `quality-*` steps after checkout/submodule update and before image builds; promotion and rollback remain immutable image pointer deploy flows; runner failure behavior already blocks subsequent steps and is now covered specifically for failing quality gates.

Kept `deploy.verify_commands` as lightweight preflight: `bash -n deploy` and `py_compile docker/api-backend/write_env.py`.
Updated Release Controller architecture docs with the canonical CI/CD flow and quality gate semantics.
Updated deploy runbook with the developer flow: local focused unit tests, Release Controller authoritative staging admission, staging smoke/integration, prod promotion from accepted immutable images.
Added guard assertions in `scripts/ci/test_release_controller_ci.py` and `scripts/ci/lint_immutable_image_workflows.py` to prevent sample/docs drift.
known_gaps: The API-host runtime config still needs to be updated with the gate list; that is owned by P003.

Full application integration tests are not yet default gates because the current full matrix still has historical red suites. They should be promoted into named gates after being made deterministic in the controller/builder environment.

The final staging release succeeded through `/v1/polls/once`, prod stayed unchanged, polling is restored, and direct manual backend/factory deploy paths remain closed.

acceptance_criteria: Release Controller config has an explicit quality-gate list with stable gate names and argv commands.
Branch release command plans contain named `quality-*` steps after checkout/submodule update and before image builds.
Promotion and rollback plans do not rebuild or rerun branch CI gates; they deploy/promote already accepted immutable image refs and smoke the target namespace.
Runner failure behavior blocks subsequent build/deploy steps when a quality gate exits nonzero.
Config validation rejects malformed quality gate definitions and empty commands.
Sample config includes meaningful default quality gates that are executable in the controller worktree.
Unit tests cover config parsing, step ordering, dry-run/real failure behavior, and retention of manual deploy guards.
Docs describe the authoritative CI/CD flow and make clear that local tests are fast feedback, not the final admission authority.
assumptions: This ticket should introduce the clean quality-gate abstraction now, while the initial default gate set can stay focused on deterministic checks that are currently green in the controller worktree.
Full application integration tests should be added as future named gates once their suites are made green and executable in the controller/builder environment.
Release Controller self-upgrade remains a separate control-plane bootstrap concern.
T000.classification_reason: Release Controller CI quality gates touch config schema, planner ordering, runner failure semantics, docs, tests, and possibly remote rollout; split keeps design/implementation and verification/deployment closure explicit.
T000.problem_definition: The release pipeline now owns backend and LLM Factory deployment, but it still treats CI-like verification as a small generic `deploy.verify_commands` preflight list. That makes the desired flow ambiguous: developers may run unit tests locally, but the authoritative staging-admission gate is not modeled as a first-class Release Controller phase with clear ordering, naming, documentation, and failure semantics.
T000.proposed_solution: Add an explicit Release Controller CI quality-gate capability. Introduce typed config for quality gates, have branch release plans execute those gates after checkout/submodule update and before image build, keep deploy smoke as a separate post-deploy stage, and ensure a failed gate stops build/deploy while recording a failed release run. Preserve existing backend/factory manual deploy rejection. Update sample config, docs, and tests so the official flow is local unit tests for fast feedback, controller CI gate for staging admission, staging smoke/integration checks, and prod promotion from the passed immutable release.
risks: Adding a heavy full-repo test gate immediately can block all releases because the current monorepo full matrix still has historical red suites.
If gates are modeled as generic verify commands only, the architecture remains ambiguous and future agents may treat smoke/preflight/CI as interchangeable.
If prod promotion reruns branch gates or rebuilds images, it breaks the immutable promote model.
T000.title: Make Release Controller enforce CI gates
T000.verification_plan: Run release-controller unit tests, release-controller CI guard tests, deploy syntax checks, relevant CI lint scripts, and focused manual negative checks for direct `services-image` / `factory-image` invocation. Inspect generated command plans in tests to prove gate ordering and prod promotion behavior.

acceptance_criteria: `ControllerConfig` exposes typed `quality_gates` and serializes them back to mappings.
`QualityGate.from_mapping` rejects missing/empty names and empty argv arrays.
Branch release plans include all quality gates with stable `quality-*` step names before build steps.
Promotion and rollback command plans contain only deploy/smoke steps and no quality/build steps.
Runner tests show a failing quality gate stops subsequent steps.
Existing planner tests for controller deploy env and manual deploy guard tests continue to pass.
assumptions: Existing `deploy.verify_commands` should remain as release preflight for now so older config keeps working, but new CI semantics live under top-level quality gates.
Initial gates will be deterministic repo checks; heavier app integration gates belong in the next child and future follow-ups.
T001.classification_reason: This child is a bounded controller code change with clear files, tests, and failure semantics; remote rollout and broader docs are separate split children.
T001.problem_definition: Release Controller needs first-class CI gate semantics instead of relying on an overloaded `deploy.verify_commands` list. The branch plan should make quality gates visible, ordered, and failure-blocking before image build/deploy, while promotion and rollback stay image-pointer operations.
T001.proposed_solution: Add a `QualityGate` model and `quality_gates` field to controller config, parse and validate named gates from config, preserve backward compatibility by allowing existing `deploy.verify_commands` to continue as preflight steps, and update branch planning so gates become `quality-<name>` command steps after source checkout/submodule update and before image build. Extend tests to verify model parsing, malformed config rejection, branch step ordering, no gates in promotion/rollback, and runner short-circuit behavior when a quality step fails.
risks: If existing config parsing becomes strict without a default, remote controller config may break on upgrade.
If gate names are not normalized, command plans may become unstable or invalid.
If verify commands and quality gates both exist, ordering must stay clear rather than becoming another ambiguous list.
T001.title: Add typed quality gate model and branch-plan execution
T001.verification_plan: Run `PYTHONPATH=novaic-release-controller python3 -m pytest novaic-release-controller/tests -q`, `python3 -m pytest -q scripts/ci/test_release_controller_ci.py`, `python3 -m pytest -q`, and a manual direct deploy guard check if touched behavior warrants it.

acceptance_criteria: Sample config has top-level `quality_gates` with stable names and executable argv commands.
Sample config keeps deploy `verify_commands` as lightweight preflight distinct from quality gates.
Docs explain local unit tests as fast feedback and Release Controller quality gates as authoritative staging admission.
Docs explain staging smoke/integration and prod promotion using the already accepted immutable release.
Repository guard tests fail if docs/sample config stop mentioning quality gates.
Release-controller tests and CI guard tests pass.
assumptions: Initial default gates should be deterministic checks that are green today: release-controller tests and release-controller CI guard tests.
Full application integration suites should become future quality gates only after the suites are made green in the controller/builder environment.
T002.classification_reason: This child is a bounded config/docs/guard update with clear files and local tests; remote rollout is isolated in P003.
T002.problem_definition: The code can now express quality gates, but the repository still needs a default controller config and docs that make the intended CI/CD flow unambiguous. Without this, future changes can leave the gate list empty or treat local tests, release preflight, staging smoke, and prod promotion as interchangeable.
T002.proposed_solution: Add meaningful deterministic quality gates to `novaic-release-controller/config.sample.json`, keep lightweight `deploy.verify_commands` as deploy preflight, update Release Controller architecture and deploy runbook docs to describe the canonical flow, and extend repository guard tests to require the sample config and docs to mention quality gates and the local-test/staging/prod responsibilities.
risks: Choosing too-heavy default gates can block every release because historical full matrix failures still exist.
Choosing too-light gates can make the new capability cosmetic.
Docs can drift unless CI guard tests pin the important contract text.
T002.title: Wire default CI gates and document the canonical flow
T002.verification_plan: Run release-controller unit tests, `scripts/ci/test_release_controller_ci.py`, relevant docs/lint scripts, `python3 -m pytest -q`, and inspect sample config parsing through `load_config`.

acceptance_criteria: Polling is paused before the quality-gate commit reaches `main`.
Intended files are committed and pushed, without staging unrelated dirty workspace changes.
A Release Controller image built from the quality-gate commit is deployed on the API host.
Remote runtime config contains `quality_gates` with `release-controller-ci` and `release-path-lints`.
A staging run through Release Controller succeeds and its command plan shows quality gates before image build/deploy.
Release Controller status has `last_error=null`, polling is re-enabled, staging health is clean, and prod current pointer remains on the prior promoted release unless a deliberate promotion is made.
Manual backend/factory deploy guards still fail direct invocation.
assumptions: The controller image can still be self-upgraded through the explicit `release-controller-image` bootstrap path.
Prod should not be promoted as part of this child unless staging verification shows a code release that the user wants promoted; the acceptance target is CI gate enforcement on staging admission.
T003.classification_reason: Remote rollout has distinct publish/readiness and API-host upgrade/verification phases; splitting avoids mixing commit hygiene with remote control-plane mutation.
T003.problem_definition: The code and docs now define Release Controller quality gates, but the running API-host controller still uses the previous image and remote config. Until the controller image and `/opt/novaic/release-controller/config.json` are upgraded, branch releases will not enforce the new gates.
T003.proposed_solution: Use the same safe control-plane rollout pattern as the previous controller-only migration: pause polling, commit and push the quality-gate code intentionally, build/push a new Release Controller image from that commit, update remote config with the quality gate list, deploy the new controller image, trigger or observe a staging release, verify the command plan contains `quality-*` steps before build/deploy, verify staging health, ensure prod pointer remains unchanged unless promoted explicitly, and resume polling.
risks: If polling is not paused before push, the old controller could process the new commit without the new gate support.
The default quality gates may fail in the remote controller container if dependencies or PYTHONPATH assumptions are wrong.
Remote config edits must preserve existing branch rules, registry paths, health URLs, and polling settings.
T003.title: Upgrade API-host Release Controller with quality gates
T003.verification_plan: Use local tests before commit, git status/diff to stage only intended files, remote `/v1/status`, remote config inspection, Docker image digest, Release Controller trigger response/run details, public staging health, prod pointer comparison, and manual direct deploy guard checks.

acceptance_criteria: Remote polling is disabled and status confirms no new autonomous release will be triggered by the push.
Local validation relevant to the quality-gate change passes.
Git staging contains only intended files for this work and no unrelated dirty files.
Commit is created on `main` and pushed to `origin/main`.
Commit id and staged file scope are recorded.
assumptions: It is acceptable to use `deploy release-controller-image` for control-plane self-upgrade/pause operations.
Parent repo is already on `main` and submodules have no intended changes for this quality-gate work.
T004.classification_reason: This is a bounded publish/readiness operation with clear validation, staging scope, and commit/push evidence; remote controller upgrade is handled by sibling P005.
T004.problem_definition: The quality-gate code must reach `main`, but the running API-host controller image does not yet understand the new gate model. Polling must be paused before the push, then only intentional files should be committed and pushed.
T004.proposed_solution: Pause API-host polling by editing the runtime config and redeploying the current Release Controller image, run local validation, stage the intended Release Controller source/config/docs/test changes plus the active problem ledger index if appropriate, commit to `main`, push to `origin/main`, and record commit/push evidence. Avoid staging unrelated pre-existing dirty docs/archive/app/start changes.
risks: Remote polling pause can be incomplete if the config changes but the controller is not restarted.
Staging unrelated dirty files could accidentally publish archived docs or user edits.
If local validation is incomplete, the pushed commit can fail remote quality gates later.
T004.title: Pause polling and publish quality-gate commit
T004.verification_plan: Use remote `/v1/status`, local test commands, `git diff --cached --name-status`, `git status --short`, `git commit`, `git push`, and `git rev-parse HEAD`.

acceptance_criteria: New controller image deployed on API host is built from commit `aaf2c823e24e970498479acda9c95ad56f03835a`.
Remote config contains `release-controller-ci` and `release-path-lints` quality gates.
Staging Release Controller run succeeds and includes `quality-release-controller-ci` and `quality-release-path-lints` before build/deploy.
Quality gate execution results are present and successful in the run output.
Staging public health is healthy after deployment.
Prod current pointer remains on commit `e4fb5740cc8481617bcd3d9f2928d7f4ebf586df` unless explicitly promoted.
Polling is re-enabled with `last_error=null`.
Direct manual `services-image` and `factory-image` calls still fail locally before remote side effects.
assumptions: The API host can build and push the Release Controller image from the pushed commit.
Staging deployment of the new commit is expected; prod promotion is intentionally not part of this ticket.
T005.classification_reason: The remaining rollout has a single ordered operational path with explicit remote checks; if a gate/runtime dependency blocks execution, a spawned child can capture that repair.
T005.problem_definition: The quality-gate commit is on `main`, but the API-host Release Controller still runs the previous image and config. The runtime must be upgraded and a staging release must prove that quality gates execute in the intended order before build/deploy.
T005.proposed_solution: Build a new Release Controller image from commit `aaf2c823e24e970498479acda9c95ad56f03835a`, push it to the internal registry as an immutable sha tag/digest, update `/opt/novaic/release-controller/config.json` with the default quality gates while preserving existing settings, deploy the new controller image, trigger a staging release for the commit, inspect the run command plan and execution results for quality gate ordering, verify staging health, confirm prod pointer did not change, re-enable polling, and verify manual deploy guards still reject direct calls.
risks: Remote quality gates may fail because the controller image or worktree lacks pytest or repo dependencies.
If remote config JSON is edited incorrectly, branch rules or smoke URLs could be damaged.
Re-enabling polling before verifying the staged run could make debugging harder.
T005.title: Deploy quality-gate controller and verify staging gate execution
T005.verification_plan: Use Docker image inspect, remote config JSON inspection, Release Controller `/v1/triggers`, `/v1/runs/{run_id}`, `/v1/status`, public health curls, prod pointer comparison, and direct guarded deploy commands.

acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile installs pytest or an equivalent package set sufficient for `python3 -m pytest`.
CI guard test asserts the Dockerfile keeps pytest available.
Local tests pass after the Dockerfile/test update.
The fix is committed and pushed without unrelated dirty files.
assumptions: A slightly larger controller image is acceptable because the controller is the CI/CD authority and must run its own gates.
T006.classification_reason: This is a narrow Dockerfile and guard-test fix with clear local verification and commit scope.
T006.problem_definition: The Release Controller image is now expected to run default pytest-based quality gates, but the image does not install pytest. The controller must carry the minimal tooling needed for its configured gate contract.
T006.proposed_solution: Update `docker/release-controller/Dockerfile` to install pytest alongside the release-controller package, add a Dockerfile invariant guard requiring pytest installation, run local release-controller tests and CI guard tests, commit and push this small fix while polling remains paused.
risks: Installing test tooling into the controller runtime image makes it slightly larger.
If future gates require additional dependencies, this fix only covers the current default gate set.
T006.title: Add test tooling to Release Controller image
T006.verification_plan: Run release-controller tests, `scripts/ci/test_release_controller_ci.py`, immutable workflow lint, root pytest, `bash -n deploy`, and inspect staged files before commit.

acceptance_criteria: Running API-host controller image is the new digest built from commit `6a74b2ac08b199a4c2c0db54c3fc31708bd59261`.
Remote config contains the two default quality gates.
`/v1/polls/once` creates a successful staging run for commit `6a74b2ac08b199a4c2c0db54c3fc31708bd59261`.
Run command plan and execution results show both quality gates before build and deploy, with exit code 0.
Staging health passes and prod pointer remains on the previous prod commit.
Polling is re-enabled with `last_error=null` and unchanged main head skip.
Direct manual backend/factory deploy commands still fail.
assumptions: Only `main` exists as a remote branch, so `/v1/polls/once` will not unexpectedly process preview or release branches.
T007.classification_reason: This follow-up has one concrete remote rollout path with explicit status, run, ordering, health, and guard checks.
T007.problem_definition: The latest `main` commit contains the quality-gate model and controller image test tooling, but the API-host Release Controller still needs to run that code and execute the gates in a real staging admission flow.
T007.proposed_solution: Build and push an immutable Release Controller image from `6a74b2ac08b199a4c2c0db54c3fc31708bd59261`, update API-host runtime config with the default quality gates while keeping polling paused, deploy the new image, execute `/v1/polls/once` so the branch polling path records the new main head and deploys staging, inspect the run plan/results for successful `quality-*` steps before build/deploy, verify staging health and prod pointer stability, then re-enable polling and verify status is clean.
risks: The quality gates may fail remotely if the controller image still lacks a tool.
The staging run may take several minutes because it builds API and Factory images.
Re-enabling polling before branch head is recorded could duplicate the staging deployment.
T007.title: Finish remote quality-gate rollout through polling path
T007.verification_plan: Inspect remote worktree, build/push image, deploy image, read remote config, call `/v1/polls/once`, fetch run details, parse command order/results, curl staging/prod health/status, and run local direct deploy guard commands.

acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile installs `pytest` and `httpx` for controller CI gate execution.
CI guard test asserts both dependencies remain in the Dockerfile.
Local tests pass.
Commit is pushed to `main` while API-host polling remains disabled.
The quality gate command succeeds manually inside the upgraded controller container.
assumptions: Installing `httpx` is the minimal correct fix because the default gate intentionally runs the controller test suite.
T008.classification_reason: This is a narrow runtime dependency repair with one Dockerfile line, one guard assertion, and direct local/remote verification.
T008.problem_definition: The remote `release-controller-ci` quality gate fails because FastAPI/Starlette `TestClient` requires `httpx`, and the Release Controller image does not install it.
T008.proposed_solution: Install `httpx` in the Release Controller Docker image together with pytest, update the Dockerfile invariant guard, run local tests, commit and push the fix, rebuild/redeploy the controller image, and verify the default quality gate command succeeds inside the container before retrying staging rollout.
risks: More test-only dependencies increase controller image size.
Future tests may introduce additional dependencies if the gate remains whole-suite based.
T008.title: Install httpx for controller CI gate
T008.verification_plan: Run local release-controller tests, CI guard tests, immutable workflow lint, root pytest, remote container manual quality gate command, and remote status polling disabled check.

acceptance_criteria: A real `/v1/polls/once` outcome for `main` succeeds for commit `491bd7e6ad55a70d96f41d0fbfea872be72e92e8`.
Quality gates are present before build/deploy in the run plan and both have successful execution evidence.
Staging health endpoint passes.
Prod current release pointer remains on `e4fb5740cc8481617bcd3d9f2928d7f4ebf586df`.
Polling is re-enabled and reports `last_error=null`.
Direct manual backend/factory deploy commands still reject without controller identity.
assumptions: Prod should not be promoted in this ticket.
T009.classification_reason: This is the final bounded verification run with explicit poll, run-detail, health, pointer, polling, and manual guard checks.
T009.problem_definition: The controller image now includes the dependencies required by the default quality gates. The final proof is a successful real branch-poll staging release for the latest main commit with quality gates executed before build/deploy.
T009.proposed_solution: Call `/v1/polls/once` while autonomous polling remains disabled, fetch the resulting run, verify plan and execution ordering, verify staging health and prod pointer stability, re-enable polling in runtime config and restart controller, confirm status is clean and main head is skipped as unchanged, then run direct manual deploy guard checks.
risks: The poll run may take several minutes because it builds and deploys API/Factory images.
If poll records the branch head but deploy health fails, rollback/pointer state must be inspected before re-enabling polling.
T009.title: Run final quality-gated staging poll
T009.verification_plan: Use `/v1/polls/once`, `/v1/runs/{run_id}`, `/v1/status`, public health curls, remote config edit/restart, and local direct deploy guard commands.
